definition:
παιδίσκη, -ης, ἡ dimin, of παῖς, 
 [in LXX for שִׁפְחָה, אָמָה, etc. ;] 
  1. a young girl, a maiden (נַעֲרָה, Rut.4:12). 
   2. Colloq., a young female slave, a maid-servant (see Kennedy, Sources, 40 f.; Deiss., LAE, 186,  332.2): Mat.26:69, Mrk.14:66, 69, Luk.12:45 22:56, Jhn.18:17, Act.12:13 16:16; of Hagar, Gal.4:22-23, 30" (LXX) ; id., metaphorically, Gal.4:31.†
 SYN.: see: παῖς  (AS)
